title: Punch-inpaint quality on dense line work
oneline: No visible smearing in composites; nearest-bleed vindicated, per-region mean adds fringe risk without benefit
summary: Ranked all 188 fills by punched % (top 18 are night fills; fire-tall.night 14.6% worst) and crop-audited 39 multi-color junction hotspots on the 13 densest pages at 3x. The shipped fills in isolation do show the predicted zipper-seam bleed, but the app composite (pen multiply / chalk screen) covers it completely at native and display scale — no wrong-color smear reaches a visible pixel. Per-region mean-color inpaint was implemented and compared same-spot: cleaner mid-stroke in isolation, but it introduces a flat-mean-vs-local-shading fringe on gradient fills, is visually identical in the composite (display-scale diffs confined to a 1px antialias band on every stroke, not junctions), and has mixed file-size impact (+38% on one page). Recommendation: keep nearest-bleed.
tried: Recomputed punched-% ranking for all 188 raw fills from the exact punch mask math
Junction hotspot finder (>=3 distinct colors around a masked pixel, tile-aggregated) on the 13 densest pages
2-4x same-spot crops: raw vs shipped fill vs simulated app composite (multiply/screen per app.css) at 39 hotspots
Per-region mean-color inpaint (connected-component labeling + label-propagating ring peel) on 8 pages
Display-scale (390/844px) composite simulation, quantitative per-pixel diff and diff heatmaps between the two variants
Baseline offline gates (drift + eye audits) on the named pages; webp size comparison at shipped encode settings
worked: Hotspot finder reliably located wheels, grilles, rail junctions, and lattices
Per-region mean inpaint implemented end-to-end, 0.16-0.67s/page, encodes cleanly
Composite simulation reproduced the app's blend math well enough to judge visibility
failed: The idea's core hypothesis: no junction smearing is visible through the overlay at any audited hotspot, native or display scale
Per-region mean introduces a new native-zoom fringe defect on shaded regions (flat mean mismatches local color at the mask boundary)
limitations: Composites simulated in sharp, not browser-rendered (lanczos3 downscale, exact CSS blend math)
Hotspot sampling stride 3px, 3 tiles/page — a single-pixel pathological junction could be missed
Deep audit covered top-10 punched pages (all night) plus 3 extra variants; thinner light masks behaved the same
Standard gates score raw fills, so they cannot detect punch-inpaint changes either way; region-mean was validated visually/quantitatively, not by gates
